ddls:
CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component */,
  "component_type" TEXT /* Type of the component */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the component was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the component */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the component */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the component */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the component */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prediction_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., discrete, continuous) */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the model */,
  "input_parameters" TEXT /* JSON string of input parameters for the model */,
  "output_parameters" TEXT /* JSON string of output parameters for the model */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model */,
  "last_trained_date" TEXT /* Date when the model was last trained */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component being predicted */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model used for prediction */,
  "predicted_failure" INTEGER /* Predicted failure of the component */,
  "predicted_utility_drop" REAL /* Predicted utility drop */,
  "predicted_rule" TEXT /* Predicted rule for the component */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the prediction */,
  "predicted_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the predicted failure or utility drop */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES prediction_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "failure_history" (
  "failure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each failure */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component that failed */,
  "failure_date" TEXT /* Date of the failure */,
  "failure_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the failure */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the failure was resolved */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Details of the resolution */,
  "maintenance_cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the maintenance to resolve the failure */,
  "downtime_duration" TEXT /* Duration of downtime due to the failure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("failure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_failure_history_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "utility_drops" (
  "drop_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each drop */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component that experienced the drop */,
  "drop_date" TEXT /* Date of the drop */,
  "drop_amount" REAL /* Amount of the drop */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the drop was resolved */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Details of the resolution */,
  "downtime_duration" TEXT /* Duration of downtime due to the drop */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("drop_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_utility_drops_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component being read */,
  "reading_date" TEXT /* Date of the reading */,
  "reading_value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_readings_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance performed */,
  "maintenance_cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the maintenance */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component being logged */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date of the log entry */,
  "log_entry" TEXT /* Log entry details */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_logs_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, data analyst, maintenance personnel) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date of the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Details of the alert message */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)
